params.name: Bronze Goblet
params.description:
On May 19, 1705 BC, the bronze goblet was cast by an artisan of the Shang Dynasty.
The bronze goblet was found in the ruins of the Erlitou Ruins in Henan province. It is the earliest dated bronze goblet found in China. The goblet has four parts: the front part is for drinking, the back part is for keeping balance and putting wine in, and the middle part connects the two parts serving as the container with three feet that are considered to be the bottom part.
